main_content: About Us Impact Reports Our small, but mighty team works tirelessly to raise awareness and make positive change. We’re proud of our work and regularly share insights and impact publicly, including organizational milestones, endometriosis research updates, and positive outcomes for our community. Impact Report 2021 In this first-ever impact report, we took a deep dive into a decade of work that has left a lasting impression on the community. Through the collective efforts of our team, volunteers, and community members, we have achieved significant progress toward our mission. The report highlights our key achievements, challenges, and learnings over a 10-year period, providing a comprehensive overview of our impact. From empowering individuals and communities, to advocating for policy changes, we have made significant strides in improving lives and creating long-lasting change. This report serves as a testament to our commitment to our mission. We hope it inspires others to join us in our efforts. Download 2021 Impact Report Impact Report 2023 This report reflects on a year of promoting awareness, advocating for policy change, and providing inclusive education, support and resources for people living with and affected by endometriosis. Download 2023 Impact Report Impact Report 2024 In 2024, we expanded our reach through national awareness, education, and support initiatives. A highlight was the launch of our first-ever pan-Canadian Know Endo digital awareness campaign in March, which amplified our message across the country. We also launched an updated website featuring the EndoHub, a central resource hub that now hosts new patient guides, our curriculum enrichment program What You Need to Know. Period, an updated surgeon directory, and many other resources. At the same time, our support groups and wellness events continued to connect thousands of people across Canada. With the continued support of the endometriosis community, we are building a future where endometriosis is better understood, diagnosed sooner, and treated with the urgency it deserves. Download 2024 Impact Report

main_content: About Us Our Story The Endometriosis Network Canada is dedicated to enhancing the lives of individuals living with endometriosis across Canada. As a registered charity in Canada founded in 2012, our measurable impact includes providing support and education to thousands of Canadians, developing dedicated programs for underserved communities, raising awareness, and impacting policy across Canada. In 2012, a group of individuals with endometriosis came together with a common goal – to improve the quality of life for people living with endometriosis across Canada. They recognized the need for resources and support, and founded The Endometriosis Network Canada as a not-for-profit organization to fulfill this need. Through their dedication and hard work, The Endometriosis Network Canada became a registered charity in 2016, and continues to inspire and support those affected by endometriosis while expanding their reach across Canada. The Endometriosis Network Canada team’s unwavering commitment to making a difference has brought hope and comfort to countless individuals affected by the chronic illness. Our mission To promote awareness, and provide inclusive education , support and resources for people living with and affected by endometriosis. Our values To cultivate an inclusive community To facilitate compassionate support To enhance and encourage knowledge To honour the individual journey To inspire hope by building an informed, compassionate, strong community. The Endometriosis Network Canada What we do Support, Education, Awareness, Hope The Endometriosis Network Canada is dedicated to providing unwavering support to, and enhancing the lives of individuals living with endometriosis across Canada. We achieve this goal by fostering an inclusive, accessible, and safer network where individuals can find support, community, and hope. Our programming includes: dedicated virtual support groups led by expert facilitators a website filled with valuable resources like our Endo Hub community engagement through social media youth educational materials like our What you need to know. Period. program dedicated programs for underserved communities like our Endo Ambassador program in-person and online educational events virtual wellness workshops awareness events like our largest fundraiser, The Endo Network’s Run to End Endo Our goal To empower and uplift those who need it most, while also creating a welcoming space that enhances knowledge, encourages advocacy, and inspires hope while respecting each individual’s unique experience of endo. Through our collective efforts, we aim to positively impact the over 2 million people living with endometriosis in Canada.
